seoTitle: Wattoning vs Wedge Island — Climate Risk Comparison | ClimateNest
seoDescription: Compare climate risk scores between Wattoning and Wedge Island. See which suburb is safer from flood, bushfire, heat, and storm hazards.
ogTitle: Wattoning vs Wedge Island: Climate Safety
ogDescription: Data-driven comparison of climate risk in Wattoning and Wedge Island.
heading: Wattoning vs Wedge Island — Which is Safer?
intro: Compare climate risk between Wattoning, Western Australia and Wedge Island, Western Australia. Our analysis uses data from BOM, CSIRO, and state hazard authorities to score each suburb across 6 climate hazards.
verdict: Wedge Island has a lower overall climate risk score (34/100) compared to Wattoning (42/100). We recommend getting an address-level report for properties in both suburbs before making a purchase decision.

faq.question: Is Wattoning or Wedge Island safer from flooding?
faq.answer: Wattoning has a flood risk score of 50/100, while Wedge Island scores 29/100. A lower score indicates lower flood risk. Get an address-level flood risk report for a specific property.

faq.question: Which suburb has lower bushfire risk?
faq.answer: Wattoning scores 54/100 for bushfire risk. Wedge Island scores 37/100. Fire risk depends on proximity to vegetation and local fire history.

faq.question: Should I buy in Wattoning or Wedge Island?
faq.answer: Both suburbs have climate considerations. Wedge Island has a lower overall climate risk score. We recommend getting a full ClimateNest report for any specific property you're considering, which includes 8 hazard scores, insurance projections, and actionable recommendations.

valueImpact.description: Properties in this area may face a 1.8% climate value drag over time due to rising insurance costs and climate adaptation expenses.
